evals.prompt: I want to add MatrixScan AR to my Cordova app. Here's my entry point: EmptyApp.js. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes and show a rectangle highlight on each detected barcode.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, writes complete BarcodeAr integration code into EmptyApp.js, and shows the setup checklist (plugins to install, cordova prepare, camera permissions, CSP, minimum plugin version 8.2).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown (mentions cordova plugin add scandit-cordova-datacapture-core and scandit-cordova-datacapture-barcode)
Setup checklist mentions minimum plugin version 8.2 for BarcodeAr
Setup checklist mentions cordova prepare after plugin install
Setup checklist mentions CSP meta tag or that camera permissions are auto-configured by the plugins
document.addEventListener('deviceready' is used as the init gate
Scandit.DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present (v8 API used)
Scandit.DataCaptureContext.forLicenseKey( is NOT present
'-- ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY HERE --' is present
Scandit.BarcodeAr.createRecommendedCameraSettings() is called
Scandit.Camera.withSettings( is called
context.setFrameSource( is called
new Scandit.BarcodeArSettings() is present
Scandit.Symbology.EAN13UPCA is enabled
Scandit.Symbology.Code128 is enabled
Only Scandit.Symbology.EAN13UPCA and Scandit.Symbology.Code128 are enabled in the primary BarcodeArSettings.enableSymbologies(...) call (semantic)
new Scandit.BarcodeAr(settings) is present (Cordova constructor used)
BarcodeAr.forContext( is NOT present
new Scandit.BarcodeArViewSettings() is present
new Scandit.BarcodeArView({ is present
barcodeArView.connectToElement( is called
new Scandit.BarcodeArRectangleHighlight( is returned from highlightProvider
highlightProvider is assigned on barcodeArView
camera.switchToDesiredState(Scandit.FrameSourceState.On) is called to start the camera
The Scandit.* global is used (no 'import ... from scandit-cordova-datacapture-*' statements in runtime code)

evals.prompt: Add MatrixScan AR to ProductListApp.js. Show a circle highlight (Dot preset) on each barcode. When the user taps a highlight, show an info annotation with the barcode data and add the barcode to the scannedProducts array.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds BarcodeAr integration to ProductListApp.js, wires a circle highlight provider, sets a uiListener for tap events, sets an annotation provider that shows info annotations, and pushes scanned barcodes to scannedProducts. All existing state and render logic is preserved.

assertions.text: document.addEventListener('deviceready' is present
Scandit.DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present
new Scandit.BarcodeArSettings() is present
new Scandit.BarcodeAr(settings) is present
new Scandit.BarcodeArView({ is present
barcodeArView.connectToElement( is called
BarcodeArCircleHighlight is used with BarcodeArCircleHighlightPreset.Dot
highlightProvider is assigned on barcodeArView with a highlightForBarcode method
annotationProvider is assigned on barcodeArView with an annotationForBarcode method
BarcodeArInfoAnnotation is constructed in annotationForBarcode
barcode.data is used as the text of a BarcodeArInfoAnnotationBodyComponent (semantic)
uiListener is assigned OR barcodeAr.addListener is used to push items to scannedProducts
Existing scannedProducts variable is referenced in the integrated code (semantic)
Existing renderProductList function is defined (semantic)
Existing onClearTapped / clear button handler is preserved
camera.switchToDesiredState( is called to start the camera

evals.prompt: In my Cordova MatrixScan AR app, I want a custom highlight provider that shows a green BarcodeArCircleHighlight with an Icon preset for barcodes whose data starts with '9', and a red BarcodeArRectangleHighlight for all other barcodes. Both should have the appropriate brush color applied.
evals.expected_output: The skill assigns a highlightProvider to barcodeArView with a highlightForBarcode method that branches on barcode.data, returning a green circle highlight or a red rectangle highlight with the correct brush in each case.

assertions.text: barcodeArView.highlightProvider is assigned
barcodeArView.highlightProvider has a highlightForBarcode function (semantic)
new Scandit.BarcodeArCircleHighlight(barcode, Scandit.BarcodeArCircleHighlightPreset.Icon) is returned for the matched condition
new Scandit.BarcodeArRectangleHighlight(barcode) is returned for the other condition
A Brush with a green hex color (e.g. '#0D853D' or '#0D853D66') is assigned for the circle highlight (semantic)
A red Brush is assigned for the rectangle highlight (e.g. Scandit.Color.fromHex('#D92121') or similar red hex)
barcode.data is inspected to determine which highlight to return
Existing integration code (context, barcodeAr, barcodeArView, camera) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to show info annotations on my Cordova MatrixScan AR integration. Each annotation should display the barcode data as the body text, a header saying 'Product' with a cyan background, and a footer saying 'Tap to dismiss'. The annotation should appear immediately when the barcode is scanned.
evals.expected_output: The skill assigns an annotationProvider to barcodeArView that constructs a BarcodeArInfoAnnotation with a header, a body component containing barcode.data, a footer, and annotationTrigger set to HighlightTapAndBarcodeScan.

assertions.text: barcodeArView.annotationProvider is assigned
barcodeArView.annotationProvider has an annotationForBarcode function (semantic)
new Scandit.BarcodeArInfoAnnotation(barcode) is constructed
Scandit.BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger.HighlightTapAndBarcodeScan is present
A BarcodeArInfoAnnotationHeader is created and assigned to annotation.header
header.text is set to 'Product' or similar
header.backgroundColor is set to a cyan or close color (e.g. #00FFFF or similar)
A BarcodeArInfoAnnotationBodyComponent is created with text set to barcode.data
annotation.body is assigned an array containing the body component
A BarcodeArInfoAnnotationFooter is created with text 'Tap to dismiss' or similar
annotation.footer is assigned
Existing integration code (context, barcodeAr, barcodeArView, camera) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to clean up properly when the user leaves the scanning screen in my Cordova MatrixScan AR app. Stop the camera, detach the BarcodeArView from its element, and null out all SDK references so they can be re-created cleanly next time.
evals.expected_output: The skill wires teardown code: camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.Off), barcodeArView.detachFromElement(), and nulling camera, barcodeArView, barcodeAr, and context.

assertions.text: camera.switchToDesiredState(Scandit.FrameSourceState.Off) is called in the teardown path
barcodeArView.detachFromElement() is called
The detachFromElement() call is awaited OR used as a promise (.then / .catch)
camera is set to null after stopping
barcodeArView is set to null after detaching
Existing integration code (context, barcodeAr, barcodeArView, camera) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my Cordova MatrixScan AR app, I want a compact status-icon annotation on each barcode. Barcodes whose data ends with '0' are flagged as close to expiry: show a yellow circle icon with an exclamation mark and the expandable text 'Close to expiry'. All other barcodes get a green circle icon with a checkmark and no expandable text. File: IntegratedApp.js
evals.expected_output: The skill assigns an annotationProvider to barcodeArView whose annotationForBarcode returns a BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation. It branches on barcode.data: a yellow ExclamationMark icon with text 'Close to expiry' for the expiry case, and a green Checkmark icon with text left null for the rest, building each icon with ScanditIconBuilder.

assertions.text: barcodeArView.annotationProvider is assigned
barcodeArView.annotationProvider has an annotationForBarcode function (semantic)
new Scandit.BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation(barcode) is constructed
annotation.icon is assigned a ScanditIcon built with new Scandit.ScanditIconBuilder()
Scandit.ScanditIconType.ExclamationMark is used for the close-to-expiry icon
Scandit.ScanditIconType.Checkmark is used for the non-expiry icon
Scandit.ScanditIconShape.Circle is used for the icon background shape
.build() is called on the ScanditIconBuilder
annotation.text is set to 'Close to expiry' for the close-to-expiry branch
annotation.text is left null (or not set) for the non-expiry branch (semantic)
barcode.data is inspected to determine which status icon to return
Existing integration code (context, barcodeAr, barcodeArView, camera) is preserved

evals.prompt: Add a popover annotation to my Cordova MatrixScan AR app. When the user taps a barcode highlight, show a popover with two buttons: an 'Accept' button with a checkmark icon and a 'Reject' button with an X-mark icon. Log the barcode data when Accept is tapped. File: IntegratedApp.js
evals.expected_output: The skill assigns an annotationProvider to barcodeArView whose annotationForBarcode builds two BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton instances (Accept with a Checkmark icon, Reject with an XMark icon), constructs a BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation with the barcode and the button array, sets annotationTrigger to HighlightTap, and wires a listener with didTapButton that logs the barcode data on the Accept button.

assertions.text: barcodeArView.annotationProvider is assigned
barcodeArView.annotationProvider has an annotationForBarcode function (semantic)
new Scandit.BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton( is used to build the Accept button
new Scandit.BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton( is used to build the Reject button
Scandit.ScanditIconType.Checkmark is used for the Accept button icon
Scandit.ScanditIconType.XMark is used for the Reject button icon
new Scandit.BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation(barcode, is constructed with the barcode and an array of buttons
Scandit.BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger.HighlightTap is assigned to annotationTrigger
popover.listener is assigned an object with a didTapButton method (semantic)
didTapButton uses buttonIndex to identify the Accept button (semantic)
barcode.data is logged when the Accept button is tapped (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeAr, barcodeArView, camera) is preserved
